name: CCLSP
what_it_does: MCP server that bridges LLM-based coding agents with Language Server Protocol servers, enabling AI assistants to jump to definitions, find references, rename symbols, and get diagnostics across codebases. Intelligently handles imprecise line/column positioning from AI by testing multiple position combinations.
use_cases: Navigate to symbol definitions without manual searching in AI coding workflows
Find all references to a symbol across a workspace from an AI agent
Perform safe workspace-wide symbol renames through natural language
Get language server diagnostics (errors, warnings) for AI-assisted debugging
Add LSP intelligence to Claude Code for any language with an LSP server
not_for: Standalone IDE replacement - requires an MCP-compatible AI client
Languages without an LSP server implementation
Non-code tasks or general-purpose AI assistance
best_when: You want Claude Code or another MCP-compatible AI agent to have precise code navigation and refactoring capabilities across multiple languages, leveraging existing LSP infrastructure.
avoid_when: Your IDE already provides sufficient LSP integration and you don't need AI-driven code navigation, or you work exclusively in languages without LSP support.
alternatives: Built-in IDE LSP support (VS Code, JetBrains)

auth.notes: No authentication required. Runs locally, communicating with locally-installed LSP servers.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Fully free and open source.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Handles AI positioning inaccuracies with multi-position testing, but error documentation is limited.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations (find_definition, find_references) are idempotent. Write operations (rename_symbol) are not reversible.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires language-specific LSP servers to be installed separately
Configuration needed per-project or per-user for LSP server commands
